---
title: "nitrix.linalg.kernel"
generated: true
signature-hash: 765bbf9263a347d5
description: "Pairwise kernel and distance functions."
listing:
  id: nitrix-linalg-kernel-symbols
  contents: "nitrix-linalg-kernel/*.qmd"
  type: table
  fields: [title, description]
  sort: "title"
  filter-ui: false
  page-size: 500
---

::: {.callout-important title="Generated reference stub — fill me"}
Auto-generated scaffold for `nitrix.linalg.kernel`. Fill the prose *outside* the
generated markers; do **not** ship as-is (SPEC §6.5). `check_reference_staleness.py` flags signature drift.
:::

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:__module__ -->
Pairwise kernel and distance functions.

The bread-and-butter primitives for kernel methods: linear,
polynomial, sigmoid, RBF / Gaussian, cosine; plus the underlying
squared-L2 distance [`linear_distance`](nitrix-linalg-kernel/linear_distance.qmd).

All accept a `theta` argument for parameterised (Mahalanobis-style)
inner products:

- `theta is None` -- the standard inner product / distance.
- `theta` a **vector** $(\dots, d)$ -- diagonal Mahalanobis;
  the kernel acts as $\sum_d \theta_d x_d y_d$.
- `theta` a **matrix** $(\dots, d, d)$ -- full Mahalanobis;
  the kernel acts as $x^{\top} \theta y$.

The squared-distance path uses the identity
$\|x - y\|^2 = \|x\|^2 + \|y\|^2 - 2\, x \cdot y$, which needs
only $(\dots, n, m)$ memory rather than materialising an
$(\dots, n, m, d)$ pairwise-difference intermediate -- a
$d$-fold reduction.  For $d$ in the thousands (covariance
vectors, voxelwise features) this is the difference between OOM and
fitting in device memory.

What we ship:

- [`linear_kernel`](nitrix-linalg-kernel/linear_kernel.qmd) -- $X_0 X_1^{\top}$, optionally
  Mahalanobis-weighted.
- [`linear_distance`](nitrix-linalg-kernel/linear_distance.qmd) -- squared L2 distance (or Mahalanobis-
  weighted squared distance), computed via the BLAS-friendly
  identity.
- [`parameterised_norm`](nitrix-linalg-kernel/parameterised_norm.qmd) -- per-row norm, optionally Mahalanobis-
  weighted.
- [`rbf_kernel`](nitrix-linalg-kernel/rbf_kernel.qmd) / [`gaussian_kernel`](nitrix-linalg-kernel/gaussian_kernel.qmd) --
  $\exp(-\gamma \|x - y\|^2)$; the two are aliases up to the
  $\gamma = \sigma^{-2}$ substitution.
- [`polynomial_kernel`](nitrix-linalg-kernel/polynomial_kernel.qmd) -- $(\gamma\, x^{\top} y + r)^{\mathrm{order}}$.
- [`sigmoid_kernel`](nitrix-linalg-kernel/sigmoid_kernel.qmd) -- $\tanh(\gamma\, x^{\top} y + r)$.
- [`cosine_kernel`](nitrix-linalg-kernel/cosine_kernel.qmd) -- linear kernel on row-normalised inputs.

For covariance / correlation kernels, use
[`nitrix.stats.pairedcov`](nitrix-stats.qmd) and [`nitrix.stats.pairedcorr`](nitrix-stats.qmd)
directly.  There are no sparse-tensor overloads here; ELL and
SectionedELL are the project's sparse formats, so build a sparse
kernel matrix via `nitrix.semiring.semiring_ell_matmul` with the
`EUCLIDEAN` semiring on a pre-thresholded adjacency.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen dims:__page__ -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `...` | anonymous leading (batch) axes |
| `n` | query points, each an `ndim`-vector coordinate (variadic) |
| `d` | spatial dimension (2 or 3) |
| `m` | rows of a multi-row contrast |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## Symbols

::: {#nitrix-linalg-kernel-symbols}
:::
